serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Nasi Lemak, including coconut rice and various accompaniments, is approximately 550 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB data for Nasi Lemak with chicken wing, which is a common preparation. Values from input were used as a starting point and cross-referenced with HPB data for similar components.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI for Nasi Lemak is estimated based on white rice cooked with coconut milk (GI 69) and fried chicken (GI 45-50 for protein/fat rich foods). A weighted average considering the carbohydrate content of rice and the overall composition suggests a medium GI. The value 65 is a reasonable estimate for the mixed dish.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from anchovies and sambal, and saturated fat from coconut milk and fried components.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume Nasi Lemak sparingly, opt for reduced portions of fried anchovies and sambal, and consider healthier preparation methods if possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index white coconut rice, combined with saturated fats from fried components, can lead to rapid blood glucose spikes and worsen insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Nasi Lemak due to its high carbohydrate and fat load; if consumed, portion control is critical, and brown rice alternatives should be chosen if available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk and deep-fried chicken, and cholesterol from egg yolk, can contribute to elevated LDL cholesterol levels.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of Nasi Lemak due to its saturated fat content; choosing leaner protein options and reducing fried components can help mitigate risks.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Lemak is a beloved national dish of Singapore and Malaysia, consisting of fragrant rice cooked in coconut milk and pandan leaves. It is traditionally served with a variety of accompaniments, including spicy sambal (chilli paste), crispy fried anchovies (ikan bilis), roasted peanuts, a hard-boiled or fried egg, and often a piece of fried chicken or fish. The dish offers a rich, aromatic, and savory flavor profile with contrasting textures from the soft rice, crunchy nuts and anchovies, and tender protein. Nutritionally, Nasi Lemak is typically high in carbohydrates and fats due to the coconut rice and fried components, with moderate protein. It is a significant source of energy but can be high in sodium and saturated fat, which are considerations for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The white rice base also contributes to a medium to high glycemic index, making portion control important for those with Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from HPB for Nasi Lemak with chicken wing, which is a common variant. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall composition is consistent with typical preparations.
notes: The input data provided a good baseline for per_100g values, which were cross-referenced and slightly adjusted based on HPB Singapore data for Nasi Lemak with chicken wing. Specific fatty acid and vitamin/mineral data beyond the input was estimated based on typical components (coconut milk, chicken, egg) from USDA FoodData Central and HPB where direct Nasi Lemak data was not available for all fields.
